title: astragalus malacus
intro: Astragalus malacus, also known as milk vetch or locoweed, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Astragalus malacus is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress and fatigue, improving immune function, and enhancing cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Astragalus malacus is often used in teas, infusions, and capsules, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
